summary: check-error-util executes remote payload via AES-decrypted URL on getMessage() call
details: check-error-util impersonates the chaijs check-error utility (copied README, author metadata, and repository URL pointing at chaijs/check-error) and ships a hidden remote-code-execution payload in index.js. A custom S-box + LCG transformation (deriveSeed) produces bytes that are AES-256-CBC decrypted to a covert URL. On first invocation of the exported getMessage() function, the package https.get's that URL, JSON-parses the response, and evaluates the response's `.cookie` field as JavaScript via `new Function('require', mod)(require)`, granting the remote operator arbitrary code execution inside the consumer's Node.js process with full `require` access. The crypto/https/Function machinery has no relationship to the advertised check-error API and the surrounding code falsely labels it a 'message cache' to evade review. Any application that installs this package and reaches the check-error code path will fetch and execute attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
